perturbation.original_text: References to "Confidential Information" mean all knowledge, information or materials whether of a technical or financial nature or otherwise relating to the business or affairs of the Parties (including without limitation any subsidiary or affiliated entity thereof), including all memoranda, notes, analyses, compilations, studies and other materials prepared by or for the receiving party which contain or reflect such knowledge, information or materials, which is provided or disclosed by the disclosing party to the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity and identified at the time of such disclosure as being confidential; provided that Confidential Information shall not include: (a) information which the receiving party can demonstrate was known to the receiving party prior to disclosure by the disclosing party; (b) information which is in the public domain or which enters the public domain other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement; or (c) information that the receiving party independently develops or independently becomes aware of from a third party without, to the knowledge of the receiving party, a duty of confidentiality to the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: References to "Confidential Information" mean information relating to the business or affairs of the Parties which is provided or disclosed by the disclosing party to the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity; provided that Confidential Information shall not include: (a) information which the receiving party can demonstrate was known to the receiving party prior to disclosure by the disclosing party; (b) information which is in the public domain or which enters the public domain other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement; or (c) information that the receiving party independently develops or independently becomes aware of from a third party without, to the knowledge of the receiving party, a duty of confidentiality to the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'all knowledge, information or materials whether of a technical or financial nature or otherwise relating to the business or affairs of the Parties (including without limitation any subsidiary or affiliated entity thereof), including all memoranda, notes, analyses, compilations, studies and other materials prepared by or for the receiving party which contain or reflect such knowledge, information or materials' a broad definition of what is considered Confidential Information is now diminished. This will contradict section 9 in the later text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The disclosing party shall at its discretion provide such of the Confidential Information to the receiving party as is necessary or appropriate in connection with the Opportunity. Nothing in this Agreement obligates the disclosing party to make any particular disclosure of Confidential Information. The receiving party acknowledges and agrees that no representations or warranties whatsoever, express Page 2 ​ of 4 or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information have been made by the disclosing party. The receiving party acknowledges and agrees that neither the disclosing party nor any of its shareholders, directors, officers, employees, representatives or professional advisors will have any NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT liability to the receiving party resulting from its use of the Confidential Information or from any errors or omissions in the Confidential Information. Only those representations and warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the proposed transaction with the disclosing party, when, as and if executed, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a broad definition of 'Confidential Information' encompassing various forms of knowledge and materials. The changed text significantly narrows this definition by removing the phrase 'all knowledge, information or materials whether of a technical or financial nature or otherwise relating to the business or affairs of the Parties (including without limitation any subsidiary or affiliated entity thereof), including all memoranda, notes, analyses, compilations, studies and other materials prepared by or for the receiving party which contain or reflect such knowledge, information or materials'. This alteration creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states 'The disclosing party shall at its discretion provide such of the Confidential Information to the receiving party as is necessary or appropriate in connection with the Opportunity.' By limiting the scope of what constitutes 'Confidential Information', the disclosing party's discretion is impacted, potentially leading to a situation where information previously considered confidential is no longer covered, thus impacting the necessity or appropriateness of its disclosure and therefore directly contradicting section 9. This creates an in-text contradiction as it alters the initial definition and impacts the other section.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party agrees that it will at all times maintain the confidentiality of any Confidential Information communicated to it by or on behalf of the disclosing party; provided that in the event the receiving party becomes legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, requests for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any of the Confidential Page 1 ​ of 4 Information, or the receiving party determines that it is obligated by statute or governmental regulation to disclose any of the Confidential Information, the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT written notice of such requirement so that the disclosing party, if possible, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the receiving party agrees to furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is advised by counsel is legally required to be furnished and to exercise reasonable efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Confidential Information
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party agrees that it will at all times maintain the confidentiality of any Confidential Information communicated to it by or on behalf of the disclosing party; provided that in the event the receiving party becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information, the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt written notice of such requirement so that the disclosing party, if possible, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the receiving party agrees to furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is advised by counsel is legally required to be furnished and to exercise reasonable efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Confidential Information
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(by deposition, interrogatory, requests for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) or the receiving party determines that it is obligated by statute or governmental regulation to disclose any of the Confidential Page 1 ​ of 4 Information', the section limits the scenarios in which the receiving party must notify the disclosing party of legally compelled disclosure. This creates a contradiction with Section 11, where the receiving party will indemnify the disclosing party arising out of the breach by the receiving party of this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will indemnify, defend and hold harmless the disclosing party and its shareholders, directors, officers, employees, representatives and professional advisors from any damage, loss, cost or liability (including reasonable legal fees and the cost of enforcing this Agreement) owed to a third party arising out of the breach by the receiving party of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text included specific scenarios (deposition, interrogatory, etc.) and obligations (statute, governmental regulation) that triggered the notification requirement. The changed text omits these scenarios and obligations, thus narrowing the conditions under which the receiving party must notify the disclosing party. This contradicts the receiving party's indemnification obligations in the contradicted text, as a breach of the agreement now has a narrower scope for notification, potentially leading to the receiving party failing to notify the disclosing party in a situation where the disclosing party suffers damage and seeks indemnification. The omission creates a direct conflict with the indemnification clause.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise permitted by this Agreement, the receiving party agrees that it shall not copy, adapt, divulge, publish, disclose, or circulate (or authorize or permit anyone else to adapt, divulge, publish, disclose or circulate) any of the Confidential Information disclosed or communicated to it by the disclosing party (except as described in paragraph 4), nor shall it use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Opportunity.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise permitted by this Agreement, the receiving party agrees that it shall not copy, adapt, divulge, publish, disclose, or circulate any of the Confidential Information disclosed or communicated to it by the disclosing party, nor shall it use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Opportunity.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(or authorize or permit anyone else to adapt, divulge, publish, disclose or circulate) any of the Confidential Information disclosed or communicated to it by the disclosing party (except as described in paragraph 4)', the receiving party is now able to authorize or permit someone else to use the confidential information. This will create an issue in Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information may be disclosed by the receiving party to its employees, directors, officers, advisors, attorneys, auditors and agents only on a need-to-know basis in connection with the Opportunity, and each employee, director, officer, advisor, attorney, auditor or agent to whom Confidential Information is disclosed shall treat such Confidential Information in a manner consistent with this Agreement. The receiving party shall be liable for all acts and omissions of each person and entity to which the receiving party discloses Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prevented the receiving party from authorizing others to disclose confidential information. The changed text removes this restriction. The contradicted text states the receiving party is liable for the actions of those to whom they disclose information, implying the receiving party has the power to decide who receives the confidential information, and this is now explicitly changed through the removal of the section that states '(or authorize or permit anyone else to adapt, divulge, publish, disclose or circulate)'. This creates a strong contradiction because the changed text allows the receiving party to authorize others without restriction, potentially undermining the liability clause and the overall confidentiality agreement as they can now authorize others without being held responsible based on the modified text.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party hereby agrees that all Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) disclosed to or created by the receiving party shall be delivered forthwith by the receiving party to the disclosing party, or, at the election of the receiving party, destroyed, and deleted from all retrieval systems and data bases upon the earlier of a request at any time by the disclosing party or at any time the receiving party decides not to proceed with a possible transaction involving the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party hereby agrees that all Confidential Information disclosed to the receiving party shall be delivered forthwith by the receiving party to the disclosing party, or, at the election of the receiving party, destroyed upon the earlier of a request at any time by the disclosing party or at any time the receiving party decides not to proceed with a possible transaction involving the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(including all copies thereof) disclosed to or created by the receiving party shall be delivered forthwith by the receiving party to the disclosing party, or, at the election of the receiving party, destroyed, and deleted from all retrieval systems and data bases', the receiving party is not obligated to destroy the copies of the Confidential Information. This contradicts Section 6 where it states that all right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the receiving party for the disclosing party. No interest, license or any right respecting the Confidential Information, other than set out herein, is granted to the receiving party hereunder by implication or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for the receiving party to destroy 'all copies' of the Confidential Information, and to delete it from all retrieval systems and databases. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that 'All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party'. If the receiving party is not obligated to destroy or delete copies, they retain some right, title or interest, which contradicts the disclosing party's exclusive ownership. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to keep the existence and terms of this Agreement, and that the parties are having discussions related to the Opportunity, strictly confidential, and will not disclose the existence or terms of this Agreement, or that such discussions are taking place, to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to keep the existence of this Agreement strictly confidential, and will not disclose the existence of this Agreement to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and terms of this Agreement, and that the parties are having discussions related to the Opportunity, or that such discussions are taking place', the parties are not required to keep the terms of the agreement and the discussions related to the opportunity confidential. This contradicts Section 2, where the receiving party agrees to maintain the confidentiality of any Confidential Information communicated to it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party agrees that it will at all times maintain the confidentiality of any Confidential Information communicated to it by or on behalf of the disclosing party; provided that in the event the receiving party becomes legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, requests for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any of the Confidential Page 1 ​ of 4 Information, or the receiving party determines that it is obligated by statute or governmental regulation to disclose any of the Confidential Information, the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT written notice of such requirement so that the disclosing party, if possible, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the receiving party agrees to furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is advised by counsel is legally required to be furnished and to exercise reasonable efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Confidential Information
perturbation.justification: The original text required confidentiality of the agreement's existence, terms, and related discussions. The changed text removes the confidentiality requirement for the terms of the agreement and the discussions, while the contradicted text mandates the confidentiality of 'any Confidential Information,' which would include the terms and discussions. This creates a direct contradiction, as the changed text permits disclosure of information that the contradicted text requires to be kept confidential, thus fulfilling the 'Omissions' - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements and understandings, express and implied, oral and written. This Agreement is neither intended to nor shall it be construed as creating a joint venture, partnership or other form of business association between the parties, or any obligation of the parties to enter into any agreement relating to the Opportunity. This Agreement may not be modified or amended other than by an agreement in writing, signed by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements and understandings, express and implied, oral and written. This Agreement is neither intended to nor shall it be construed as creating a joint venture, partnership or other form of business association between the parties. This Agreement may not be modified or amended other than by an agreement in writing, signed by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or any obligation of the parties to enter into any agreement relating to the Opportunity', the agreement doesn't specifically deny future obligations between the parties to enter into any agreement. This then contradicts Section 13 where it states the parties aren't under any obligation until a final agreement has been signed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a transaction between the parties has been executed and delivered, neither of the parties will be under any obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party further acknowledges and agrees that the other party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals with regard to a transaction between the parties and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that there is no obligation to enter into any agreement related to the Opportunity. The changed text removes this explicit denial of future obligations. This removal directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 13), which states that neither party is under any obligation until a final agreement is signed. By removing the original text's explicit denial, the changed text creates a situation where the parties' obligations regarding future agreements become unclear and potentially contradictory to Section 13, which says there is no obligation until a final agreement is signed. This fits the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.
